{"society_power": 4.5, "state_power": 1.0, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": -2.5, "key_event": "State collapse (1991); clan norms and warlords fill vacuum", "reasoning": "The 1991 fall of Siad Barre eliminated the central state, collapsing state power to near-zero as failed UN interventions and warlord fragmentation left no institutional replacement. Society reverted to clan-based customary authority (xeer/elders), a restrictive \"cage of norms\" that provided real localized organization\u2014especially in Somaliland/Puntland\u2014but was militarized and famine-shattered, yielding only a modest net rise."}
